free_text: for the thermal neutron sub-barrier fission of
Np-237 and above-the-barrier fission of U-235.-
Inclined beam hole IH1 of the
high flux reactor. Thermal flux 5.E+9 n/cm*cm*sec,
-For the fragment energy calibration a sample of
a similar backing as the Np-sample backing was used.
A Fast coincidence (30 nsec) is used to
detect the two fission fragment kinetic energies.
Simultaneously the difference of time of flight
of the fission fragments was also recorded. If the
difference between this measured TOF-difference and
the TOF-difference calculated from the measured
kinetic energies is less than a certain value, the
measurement is accepted. This procedure eliminates
contamination of the spectra from poorly measured
high yield events (e.g. from backscattered fission
supplied by ORTEC, 2 cm diameter, collimated to 1.5 cm
diameter with appropriate diaphragms to avoid edge
effects. Coincidences between the two complementary
-Fragment pulse heights were converted into fragment
through an iterative process using mass-dependent
energy calibration method of Schmitt, Neiler et al,
the mass and momentum conservation relations and the
